body:
Tom,

Overnight totals came in at 14 inches up on the tram, wind loading heavy on north and east aspects above 9,500 ft. GNFAC has it at Considerable. I'm holding Corbet's and the Cirque closed until we get control work done this morning. Rest of the mountain should open on schedule.

Going through advisory with Jake Tanner at 6:30 briefing, will confirm final route status after that. Wanted you to have the heads up early since you asked last time.

Jeff

Jeff,

Thanks for the follow-up, good info. Appreciate you flagging Corbet's and the Cirque ahead of time — makes sense given the loading pattern you're describing. Hope control work goes smooth and you don't lose too much of the morning to it.

Let me know once Jake and the team finalize route status, and give my best to Cody. Stay safe out there.

Tom

Tom,

Following up on the ticket from Tuesday. The shift report tool is still dropping route status when I toggle Cody Bowl and Rendezvous Bowl to "open" back to back. I log both closed after avy control, open one, close the other, and by the time I submit, the second one reverts to whatever it was that morning. Jake Tanner's had two other patrollers report the same thing this week.

Workaround right now is submitting one route at a time with a save in between, which adds probably ten minutes to every closing report. Not sustainable once we're into full season traffic.

Can you check if this is a known bug or something specific to our resort's config?

Jeff Peterson
Teton Summit Ski Patrol

Jeff,

Thanks for the follow-up. Pulled the ticket and I see the pattern — looks like a caching issue on the multi-route batch save, not specific to Teton Summit. Engineering confirmed it's affecting a few other resorts running the same version.

Fix is queued for the patch going out Thursday night. Until then, your one-at-a-time workaround is the right call. I'd also recommend refreshing the page between submissions rather than navigating back — should cut down on the revert behavior.

I'll email you directly once the patch is live so you can confirm on your end before I close this out. Appreciate you flagging it with specifics, makes it a lot easier to track down.

Tom Peterson
Support

Tom,

Good to hear it's not just us. Tried the refresh-between-submissions trick this morning on the avy advisory update and it held — both routes stayed correct through the shift.

Go ahead and let me know when Thursday's patch is confirmed live. I'll run the batch toggle test myself before you close the ticket, want to make sure it's solid before the rest of the team relies on it again.

Thanks for turning this around quick.

Jeff

Hey Tom,

Following up on the patrol schedule export issue I flagged last week. The weekly review still isn't matching what Jake Tanner has on the master board — Thursday shows me on Bear Ridge and off on Cirque, but the printed sheet at the shack has it flipped. Second week in a row this has happened.

Can you check if the sync between the scheduling tool and the print feed is dropping updates after Tanner locks the week? I need this clean before Sunday's review meeting.

Thanks,
Jeff

Hi Jeff,

Thanks for flagging this again — sorry it's still causing headaches. I pulled the logs and you're right, there's a sync delay when a schedule gets locked and then edited within the same hour. The print feed grabs a cached version before the edit propagates.

I'm pushing a fix today that forces a re-sync on any post-lock edit. Can you have Jake Tanner re-save this week's schedule after 3pm so it picks up the patch? I'll also flag your account to double-check next week's export before it prints.

Let me know if Sunday's sheet still looks off.

Best,
Tom

Tom,

Had Tanner re-save around 4. Checked the sheet this morning — Bear Ridge and Cirque both showing right now. Also noticed the route-status column (open/closed) is syncing correctly for the first time in a while, so whatever you did helped more than one thing.

One more ask: any way to get an email alert when Tanner locks the week, instead of me checking manually? Would save some back and forth, especially on avalanche advisory mornings when I'm slammed before first chair.

Jeff

Jeff,

Glad it's holding. Good catch on the route-status column — that was tied to the same caching bug, so one fix covered both.

On the lock alert: yes, that's doable. I'll set up an automated email to you whenever Tanner finalizes the week's schedule, sent within a few minutes of the lock. Should be live by Friday. If it's not showing up by then, just reply here and I'll check the trigger.

Appreciate you sticking with this through the back and forth.

Tom

Hey Tom,

Following up on the strength program we set up last month at Snow King Fitness. Squats and pull days are going fine, but the 5am slot keeps colliding with avalanche advisory checks — I need to be at the forecast desk by 6 most mornings during patrol season. Can we shift my sessions to the 7pm block instead? Tuesdays and Thursdays work best around my patrol shifts.

Also, my knees are feeling the box jumps more than they should. Wondering if there's a lower-impact substitute for the plyo block given I'm on my feet all day on skis.

Thanks,
Jeff Peterson

Jeff,

Good to hear from you. We can move you to the 7pm Tue/Thu slot starting next week — I'll update the front desk roster so they're expecting you.

On the knees: let's swap box jumps for kettlebell swings and step-ups for a few weeks. Lower impact, still builds the explosive power you want for patrol work. I'll have a revised program printout ready when you come in.

See you Thursday.

Tom

Hey Tom,

Following up on ticket #4482. The Gmail API token for the advisory script is still throwing invalid_grant every other morning. I've got it set to auto-send the overnight snowfall and avalanche advisory to patrol dispatch at 5am before shift, and when it fails I end up hand-typing the report from my phone in the locker room. Not ideal.

I re-ran the OAuth consent flow like you suggested last week, refresh token looked fresh, but it died again this morning. Anything on your end about scope changes or token expiry policy that might explain the pattern?

Jeff

Jeff,

Thanks for the follow-up. Pulled the logs on our end. The invalid_grant is a refresh token expiring after 7 days — your OAuth consent screen is still in "Testing" mode in Google Cloud Console, which caps refresh tokens at that window instead of issuing long-lived ones.

Two options: publish the app (needs verification if you're requesting sensitive scopes) or, since this is just for you, add your account explicitly under Audience > Test Users with no expiry limit — that's not actually available, so realistically you want to move it to In Production. For a single-user script the review is usually fast since you're only requesting gmail.send.

Can you confirm which scope you're requesting? If it's gmail.send only, I can walk you through publishing without needing Google's full verification, since that scope is low-risk.

Tom

Tom,

That was it. Scope was just gmail.send. Moved the app to Production this morning, reauthorized once, and the 5am advisory run went out clean today — no manual retyping in the locker room. Ran it past Jake Tanner too since dispatch depends on it, he's fine with the setup now that it's stable.

Appreciate you digging into the logs instead of just having me re-auth blind again. Ticket can close.

Jeff

Hey Tom,

Following up on ticket #4471 from last week. I set up a script using the Google Calendar API to pull my weekly patrol schedule from our internal roster tool and push it into my personal calendar — helps me keep shift starts, route openings, and my Snow King Fitness sessions from colliding. It worked fine for about four days, then started throwing 403 errors on every insert call yesterday morning.

I didn't change anything on my end. Same service account, same calendar ID. Only thing different is I added a second calendar for Cody so we can see each other's days off without her needing her own dev key.

Can you check if something changed on quota limits or scopes? I've got the early advisory check tomorrow at 5am and would like the calendar back before then.

Thanks,
Jeff Peterson

Hi Jeff,

Thanks for the ticket number, pulled it up. A 403 right after adding a second calendar usually means a scope or permissions issue rather than quota — quota errors come back as 429s with a different reason code.

Couple things to check on your end:
1. Does the service account have Editor access explicitly granted on Cody's calendar (not just yours)? Adding a calendar ID to your script doesn't auto-grant access.
2. Can you send the exact error body? Look for a "reason" field — should say something like insufficientPermissions or notFound.
3. What scopes did you request when you generated the OAuth token — calendar vs calendar.events?

If you can paste the raw JSON error response from your logs, I can tell you in one look what's wrong. Should be a quick fix once I see it.

Best,
Tom

Tom,

Good catch. Pulled the log — here's the relevant chunk:

{"error":{"code":403,"message":"The caller does not have permission","status":"PERMISSION_DENIED"}}

Checked Cody's calendar sharing settings and yeah, I never added the service account email there, just mine. Rookie mistake, was moving fast between patrol shifts. Scope on the token is calendar.events, not full calendar, if that matters.

One more thing while I've got you — is there a way to batch insert so I'm not hitting the API once per shift? I'm pushing about 12 events a week between my schedule, hers, and the avy course block I've got coming up. Don't want to trip anything if I add more calendars later, maybe for my brother Kyle's shifts too.

Jeff

Jeff,

That confirms it — PERMISSION_DENIED with that message is almost always a sharing gap, exactly what you found. Once you add the service account as an Editor on Cody's calendar it should resolve immediately, no token or scope changes needed. calendar.events scope is fine for what you're doing, no need to widen it.

On batching: yes, use the batch endpoint or events.insert with a batch HTTP request — up to 50 operations per batch call. At 12 events a week you're nowhere near quota limits (default is 1,000,000 queries/day, 500/100sec/user), so you could even add several more calendars for Kyle or the rest of your patrol crew without worrying about throttling.

Go ahead and test the fix, then let me know if the 403 clears. I'll leave ticket #4471 open until you confirm, then close it out.

Tom

Hi Tom,

Following up on the Google Drive API ticket from last week. Still getting the 403 quota errors when our script pushes the nightly avalanche advisory PDFs and snowpack observation sheets up to the shared patrol drive. It's failing around 2am when the export job runs, which means I'm walking into dispatch some mornings with no synced report.

I regenerated the service account key like your last email suggested, updated the credentials file, and it worked for about two days before the same error came back. Quota page in the console shows we're nowhere near the daily limit, so I'm stuck on why it's throttling.

Can you check if there's a project-level restriction on our end, or if this is a known bug with the Drive API right now? Need this reliable before the season really ramps up here at Teton Summit.

Thanks,
Jeff Peterson

Hi Jeff,

Thanks for the detail, that helps narrow it down. Pulled your project logs and I see it — you've got two OAuth clients tied to the same service account, and the older one still has a stale scope from before we split the read/write permissions last quarter. That's what's tripping the 403, it's not actually a quota issue, it's a permissions mismatch that only shows up as a quota error in the response.

I've revoked the old client and issued a fresh key scoped correctly for Drive file write access. Download it from the console under your project settings and swap it into your script's credentials path. Should hold steady through nightly runs now.

Run your export job manually once tonight after you swap the key and let me know if the PDF and observation sheet both land in the shared folder clean. If it holds for 48 hours I'll close this ticket out on our end.

Best,
Tom Peterson

Tom,

Following up on ticket #4417 from last week. The WhatsApp API integration for our patrol dispatch alerts is still dropping messages intermittently. We're using it to push overnight snowfall and avalanche advisory summaries to the patrol group each morning before shift briefing, so reliability matters.

Specifically: messages queue fine but about 1 in 5 never deliver to numbers on the list. No error shown on our end. Jake Tanner, our patrol director, wants this solid before we roll it out resort-wide for route-open/close notices.

Can you check the delivery logs on your side for account ID TSR-0092? Happy to send timestamps if useful.

Jeff Peterson
Teton Summit Resort Ski Patrol

Jeff,

Thanks for the follow-up. I pulled account TSR-0092 and I do see a pattern — several of the failed sends are hitting numbers that aren't opted in to the WhatsApp Business template yet, which the API silently drops instead of erroring. That's likely your 1-in-5.

A couple things I need from you: a current list of the patrol phone numbers you're sending to, and confirmation you're using the approved message template (not free-form text) for the morning advisory push. If it's free-form, that's outside the 24-hour session window and will get dropped for anyone who hasn't messaged you first.

Send those over and I'll re-check against our delivery logs.

Tom Peterson
Support, WhatsApp Business API

Tom,

That explains it. We were sending free-form — nobody set up a template. Attached is the current patrol roster with numbers, 14 total. Most of them have never messaged the business number first, so that tracks with what you're seeing.

Can you walk me through setting up an approved template for the daily advisory? Something like a short snowpack/avy rating summary with a link to the full report. If we can get that live before next week's schedule review, I can present it to Jake Tanner as ready to go.

Appreciate the quick diagnosis. Beats losing messages after already being deep in a route briefing.

Jeff

Tom,

Following up on ticket #4471 from Tuesday. The overnight snowfall feed from the Rendezvous study plot was still blank on the advisory dashboard at 5:40am today, right before our route-opening call. Had to call it with manual stake readings from the summit and Cody Bowl gate instead.

This is the second time in a week. Any update on root cause? I need this reliable before 6am, not after.

Jeff

Jeff,

Thanks for flagging, and sorry for the repeat. Pulled the logs — the sensor at Rendezvous had an intermittent power fault overnight, so the API timed out pulling new readings and the dashboard just showed the last cached value as blank instead of stale-labeled. We're deploying a fix today that flags data as "stale" rather than dropping it silently.

Can you confirm the exact station ID you're pulling from in the advisory app? Want to make sure I'm patching the right feed before I close this out.

Tom

Tom,

Station ID is RVZ-01, upper Rendezvous plot. That's the one feeding our morning avalanche advisory brief, so silent failure isn't acceptable — a blank reading looks the same as "no new snow," and that's how people make bad calls on route openings.

Beyond the stale flag, can you add a redundant source, even a rough tie-in to the Jackson Hole snotel site, so we're not fully dependent on one sensor? I'll take a delayed number over a missing one every time.

Jeff

Jeff,

Fix is live as of this morning. RVZ-01 now flags stale data with a timestamp instead of going blank, and I've added the Rendezvous SNOTEL feed as a fallback source — dashboard will auto-switch if RVZ-01 drops out again.

Ran a test pull at 4am today and both sources reported clean. I'll keep an eye on it through the week and ping you directly if anything looks off before your morning brief. Closing ticket #4471 out.

Tom

Hey Dad,

Quick follow-up on the shift stuff we talked about at the house. Route sheet for tomorrow: Corbet's and Rendezvous Bowl stay closed till we get eyes on the new load, everything else is open. I'm on first report at 6, then sweeping the tram side with Jake Hoffman.

Jake Tanner moved my next two shifts around for the schedule review, so I'm actually off Thursday now instead of Wednesday. Works out fine since Cody and I already had Thursday blocked for our day off.

Let me know if you still want me to swing by and look at the tractor before the weekend. Otherwise I'll call Sunday like usual.

Jeff

Jeff,

Good to hear Corbet's is getting held back, sounds like you're staying on top of it. No rush on the tractor, whenever you've got time between shifts is fine.

Your mother wants to know if you and Cody are still coming up for dinner on your day off, or if training's got you wiped out again. Let us know either way.

Talk Sunday.

Dad

Tom,

Following up on the schedule review from Tuesday. I need next week's rotation finalized before Thursday — Jake Tanner wants route assignments posted early since we've got a storm cycle moving in and I want experienced patrollers on the upper mountain zones.

Can you confirm whether Jake Hoffman is still down for Saturday? If he's out I need to shuffle Cody Bowl and Rendezvous coverage. Also flag me if there's a conflict with my Snow King Fitness block Wednesday morning — I'd rather move that than leave a gap on the roster.

Appreciate you staying on top of this.

Jeff

Jeff,

Got your note. Jake Hoffman is confirmed for Saturday, no changes there. I moved your Wednesday fitness block to the 6am slot so it doesn't overlap with route opening — let me know if that doesn't work with Snow King's hours.

One open item: we're still short a patroller for Sunday afternoon on the lower mountain. Do you want me to pull someone from the on-call list, or are you planning to cover it yourself?

Will get the full rotation posted by Thursday morning either way.

Tom

Tom,

6am works fine, Snow King opens at 5. For Sunday, pull from on-call — I've got the day off with Cody and I'd like to keep it that way for once.

Otherwise the rotation looks solid. Thanks for turning this around fast.

Jeff

Hi Tom,

Following up on the strength block we set up last month at Snow King Fitness. I've been in three times a week around patrol shifts, but this week's schedule got rearranged for the weekly patrol review and I missed the Thursday session.

Can you tell me if there's a make-up slot, or should I just fold it into next week's plan? Also wondering if the trainer can adjust the loading — my hips are feeling the boot-pack days more than usual this season.

Thanks,
Jeff Peterson

Jeff,

Good to hear from you. No problem on the missed Thursday — we can slot you in Saturday morning instead, gym's quieter then anyway. I'll flag the trainer about the hip loading; makes sense given how much time you're spending on your feet for avalanche work.

One thing — do you want us to keep your program flexible around patrol on-call days, or lock in fixed days each week? Easier for us to plan the trainer's schedule either way, just let us know your preference.

Best,
Tom Peterson
Snow King Fitness

Tom,

Flexible is better. Patrol schedule shifts week to week depending on avalanche advisory workload, so a fixed slot won't hold up. Saturday morning works for the make-up this week.

Also, if it's not too much trouble, could you send over a version of the program I can do at home or at the patrol shack on short-notice days? Overnight snowfall checks sometimes eat my whole morning and I don't want to lose the training entirely.

Appreciate the flexibility.

Jeff

Jeff,

Done — Saturday morning is booked, and I've had the trainer put together a shortened bodyweight version you can run in 20 minutes, no equipment needed. Emailed it separately as a PDF.

We'll keep your slots flexible going forward. Just text or email us when your week firms up and we'll fit you in where we can. Good luck out there this season.

Tom Peterson
Snow King Fitness
